label: Python function (named callable, incl. methods and nested functions)
gloss: A Python function: a named callable introduced by a def (or async def) statement; includes methods and nested functions; a lambda is not one.
notes: Structural (non-NSM) definition; see data/code-v0/README.md for the profile decision.

definition.definition: A Python function: a named callable introduced by an ast.FunctionDef or ast.AsyncFunctionDef node. Methods (functions whose immediate lexical parent is a class body) and nested functions (whose immediate lexical parent is another function body) are python-functions. An unnamed lambda expression is NOT a python-function.
